finding: Perceiving a harmful action as intentional rather than accidental increases observer motivation to assign blame, measured via free choice of blaming task over alternatives and persistence in blaming behavior despite increasing effort cost, compared to accidental harm scenarios.

The mere possibility that a third party can intervene to reward cooperation and punish competition increases cooperation among disputants, with cooperation rates rising monotonically as intervention probability increases from 10% to 90%, while self-interested third parties intervene only when potential gains outweigh losses.

Postural expansiveness (opening the body in space) increases romantic attraction in both speed-dating and online dating contexts, with the effect mediated by perceptions of dominance; each unit increase in expansiveness nearly doubles odds of receiving a 'yes' response from a dating partner.

Higher income predicts lower generosity only in high-inequality regions; in low-inequality regions, higher income predicts equal or greater generosity, showing that the relationship between income and prosocial behavior depends on contextual inequality levels rather than being a stable individual trait.

White medical students and residents who endorse false beliefs about biological differences between races rate black patients' pain as lower and recommend less accurate treatment compared to those who do not endorse such beliefs, with racial bias in pain perception mediating racial bias in treatment recommendation accuracy.

People choosing to withhold personal information are judged more negatively than those admitting to unsavory behavior, and this penalty for withholding stems from inferences about trustworthiness; decision-makers fail to anticipate this and choose to hide even when disclosure would improve their social evaluation.
